title: @island
description: The @island directive creates isolated regions within a component that update independently, without re-rendering the entire component.

sections.title: #Basic usage
sections.content:
Wrap any portion of your template with @island to create an isolated region:
When the "Refresh" button is clicked, only the island re-renders—the rest of the component remains untouched.

examples.code:
@island
Revenue: {{ $this->revenue }} 
@endisland

sections.title: #Lazy loading islands
sections.content:
Defer an island's initial render until after the page loads using the lazy parameter:
The island displays a loading state initially, then fetches its content in a separate request.
By default, lazy waits until the island is visible in the viewport. Use defer to load immediately after page load:

examples.code: {{-- Loads when scrolled into view --}}@island(lazy: true) <!-- ... -->@endisland {{-- Loads immediately after page load --}}@island(defer: true) <!-- ... -->@endisland

sections.title: #Custom loading states
sections.content: Use @placeholder to customize what displays while loading:

examples.code:
@island(lazy: true) @placeholder
@endplaceholder 
Revenue: {{ $this->revenue }}
@endisland

sections.title: #Named islands
sections.content:
Give islands names to target them from elsewhere in your component:
The wire:island directive scopes updates to specific islands.

sections.title: #Why use islands?
sections.content:
Islands provide performance isolation without the overhead of creating separate child components, managing props, or dealing with component communication.
Use islands when:
Learn more about islands →

examples.code: @island( ?string $name = null, bool $lazy = false, bool $defer = false,) <!-- Content -->@endisland
